hypothesis: Enhancing aromatic complementarity at position 5 and hydrophobic packing at position 11 will improve SSTR2-specific binding while reducing off-target affinity (SSTR3/SSTR5), thereby increasing selectivity margin.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current designs are achieving good selectivity but are structurally suboptimal. Tightening the ddG threshold and increasing candidate diversity should help converge on structurally stable and selective SSTR2 ligands.

proposed_changes.rationale: Tightening the ddG threshold will filter out structurally unstable designs and improve overall binding affinity for SSTR2 while maintaining selectivity.
Increasing the number of candidates will expand the diversity of the design space and increase the likelihood of finding structurally sound and selective candidates.
